mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the housing market signals reduced demand for new construction, leading to increased inventory and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 units in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to increased economic growth and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in pre-expansion periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index signals reduced inflationary pressures, leading to increased monetary policy easing and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL falls below 200 in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the labor market signals increased consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in pre-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the retail sales signals reduced consumer demand, leading to increased inventory and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below 400000 in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to increased economic growth and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in pre-expansion periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 0.1% in 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
